generatedSentence: Bobby, a humble musician, found his recent promotion to a prestigious title greatly affected his life, making him feel like an impostor; his paycheck felt gimmicky. His modest thatch-roofed home, where rainwater was collected for sanitary mouthwashes, offered a view of an unusual landscape, an abyss. He noticed an abnormal sunstrike over a distant wall, considering it a scale. The complexity of his feelings, perhaps a narcissist's influence, made him greatly doubt either his talent or his future. He'd often ride his bicycle past fields of grapes and obeches, trying to habilitate his mind. A grumpy widower, a retired nurse, developed an underdog attitude after countless journeys, realizing the universe was undefined. He possessed a strange foresight, predicting unusual births and cars on a rampage under an artificial aurora. He tried to empower others, but his real version of events, like marshmallow fluff, was often dismissed. The adjustments needed seemed insurmountable, like trying to find a groove while dodging onions that litters the paddy field with a jackrabbit. Their journey trekked them to a faraway ballpark, a private inferno where a mammoth cyborg, with webbed limbs and incredible craftsmanship, caused trouble. It seemed unlucky, as if they were plucked from their prospects; its movements, a violent dance, made the ground wobble. The cyborg, showing no notions of empathy, emitted strange farts, its vocabulary consisting of versatile, guttural roars. They realized this wasn't one of the 'normals'; its glowing orbs illuminated unswept objects and discarded eardrops near a rabies warning sign. They had to bypass its defenses. The ethical dilemma of its creation, by illicit owners of obscure securities, became clear as the injured cyborg was initializing; its nationality was uppercase 'UNDEFINED'. The nerdy fiddler, Bobby, used a talking telephone to alert authorities, dispelling any doubt. The time for showbiz was over. A crystalline morsel of truth emerged from the umbrous depths, as the adjoint fairies, like oxen, worked to restore peace.

imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, vibrant neon futurism, bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting, thick white gutters, clean black borders. Protagonist is a slender man with a vintage gramophone horn helmet and glowing blue gauntlets. TOP-LEFT PANEL: Protagonist Bobby on a futuristic bicycle, looking towards a distant sunstrike over a city skyline, detailed close-up shot of his intense expression. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: An aerial view of a neon-lit city street, cars on a rampage under a holographic aurora, a grumpy nurse widower in a hover-kayak observing the chaos, medium shot. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: A colossal webbed cyborg battling in a destroyed ballpark, glowing orbs highlighting its immense craftsmanship, low-angle dynamic shot. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: Protagonist Bobby holding a retro-futuristic telephone, speaking with a determined expression, a crystalline data interface projected around him, medium close-up.

definitions.definition: A person or object that is not normal.
Not conforming to rule or system; deviating from the usual or normal type.
Of or pertaining to that which is irregular, in particular, behaviour that deviates from norms of social propriety or accepted standards of mental health.
To influence or alter.
definitions.example: The experience affected me deeply.

definitions.definition: To move to emotion.
definitions.example: He was deeply affected by the tragic ending of the play.

definitions.definition: Of an illness or condition, to infect or harm (a part of the body).
definitions.example: Hepatitis affects the liver.

definitions.definition: To dispose or incline.
To tend to by affinity or disposition.
To assign; to appoint.
To make a show of; to put on a pretense of; to feign; to assume. To make a false display of.
definitions.example: He managed to affect a smile despite feeling quite miserable.

definitions.definition: To aim for, to try to obtain.
To feel affection for (someone); to like, be fond of.
To show a fondness for (something); to choose.
One's mood or inclination; mental state.
A desire, an appetite.
A subjective feeling experienced in response to a thought or other stimulus; mood, emotion, especially as demonstrated in external physical signs.
Hell; the bottomless pit; primeval chaos; a confined subterranean ocean.
(frequently figurative) A bottomless or unfathomed depth, gulf, or chasm; hence, any deep, immeasurable; any void space.
Anything infinite, immeasurable, or profound.
Moral depravity; vast intellectual or moral depth.
An impending catastrophic happening.
The center of an escutcheon.
The abyssal zone.
A difference, especially a large difference, between groups.
A small, round, smooth-skinned edible fruit, usually purple, red, or green, that grows in bunches on vines of genus Vitis.
A woody vine that bears clusters of grapes; a grapevine; of genus Vitis.
A dark purplish-red colour, the colour of many grapes.
Grapeshot.
A mangy tumour on a horse's leg.
A person's head.
To pick grapes.
(of livestock) To develop tubercules as a result of tuberculosis.
To develop a texture with small grape-like clusters of a contaminant or foreign substance.
(north) To grope.
Triplochiton scleroxylon, a tropical tree of Africa.
The wood of this tree; whitish yellow in colour and easy to process.
Someone who attempts to deceive by using an assumed name or identity.
A sprite or animation integrated into a three-dimensional scene, but not based on an actual 3D model.
A portion of land or territory which the eye can comprehend in a single view, including all the objects it contains.
A sociological aspect of a physical area.
A picture representing a real or imaginary scene by land or sea, the main subject being the general aspect of nature, as fields, hills, forests, water, etc.
The pictorial aspect of a country.
A mode of printing where the horizontal sides are longer than the vertical sides
A space, indoor or outdoor and natural or man-made (as in "designed landscape")
A situation that is presented, a scenario
definitions.example: The software patent landscape has changed considerably in the last years

definitions.definition: To create or maintain a landscape.
An advancement in rank or position.
definitions.example: I'll have to give myself a promotion!

definitions.definition: Dissemination of information in order to increase its popularity.
definitions.example: the promotion of the idea of global warming in schools

definitions.definition: An event intended to increase the reach or image of a product or brand.
definitions.example: The price cut is serving as a promotion of the manufacturer's new beverage varieties.

definitions.definition: Forward motion. (Contrast remotion.)
A police officer.
A railway signaller.
A vehicle that has two wheels, one behind the other, a steering handle, and a saddle seat or seats and is usually propelled by the action of a rider’s feet upon pedals.
A traveling block used on a cable in skidding logs.
The best possible hand in lowball.
A motorbike.
(usually in compounds specifying a context) a slut; a promiscuous woman
To travel or exercise using a bicycle.
Straw, rushes, or similar, used for making or covering the roofs of buildings, or of stacks of hay or grain.
Any of several kinds of palm, the leaves of which are used for thatching.
A buildup of cut grass, stolons or other material on the soil in a lawn.
(by extension) Any straw-like material, such as a person's hair.
Rainfall.
Water (for a house etc) sourced from rain which has not joined a spring, stream or river, pond, lake or sea.
Sanitary towel.
Of, or relating to health.
Clean and free from pathogens; hygienic.
To treat a corpse with preservatives in order to prevent decomposition.
To perfume or add fragrance to something.
(after a negative) As well.
definitions.example: I don't like him, and I don't like her either.

definitions.definition: One or other of two people or things.
definitions.example: He made me two offers, but I did not accept either.

definitions.definition: Both, each of two or more.
Introduces the first of two (or occasionally more) options or possibilities, the second (or last) of which is introduced by “or”.
definitions.example: Either you eat your dinner or you go to your room.

definitions.definition: A person who has never had sexual intercourse, or sometimes, one who has never engaged in any sexual activity at all.
(early Christian Church) a woman noted for religious piety who has never been married.
One who has never used or experienced a specified thing.
definitions.example: I've never eaten tofu before – you could say I'm a tofu virgin.

definitions.definition: Any of several species of gossamer-winged butterflies of the family Lycaenidae.
A female insect producing eggs from which young are hatched, though there has been no fecundation by a male; a parthenogenetic insect.
A rampart of earth, stones etc. built up for defensive purposes.
A structure built for defense surrounding a city, castle etc.
definitions.example: The town wall was surrounded by a moat.

definitions.definition: Each of the substantial structures acting either as the exterior of or divisions within a structure.
definitions.example: We're adding another wall in this room during the remodeling.  The wind blew against the walls of the tent.

definitions.definition: A point of desperation.
A point of defeat or extinction.
An impediment to free movement.
definitions.example: A wall of police officers met the protesters before they reached the capitol steps.

definitions.definition: A type of butterfly (Lasiommata megera).
(often in combination) A barrier.
definitions.example: a seawall;  a firewall

definitions.definition: A barrier to vision.
Something with the apparent solidity and dimensions of a building wall.
definitions.example: a wall of sound;  a wall of water

definitions.definition: A divisive or containing structure in an organ or cavity.
(auction) A fictional bidder used to increase the price at an auction.
A doctor who tries to admit as few patients as possible.
A line of defenders set up between an opposing free-kick taker and the goal.
A personal notice board listing messages of interest to a particular user.
To enclose with, or as if with, a wall or walls.
definitions.example: He walled the study with books.

definitions.definition: To enable one to function in a given manner; to make one capable of performing a given function or of conducting something; to make one fit to fulfill a given purpose or competent to act within a particular role.
To qualify oneself, through a demonstration of ability, to function in a certain capacity or to act within a certain role.
In European institutions of higher education, to qualify as an instructor or professor, usually by defending a dissertation or similar project.
To supply money to work a mine.
Qualified or entitled.
The state of being complex; intricacy; entanglement.
That which is and renders complex; intricacy; complication.
One who shows extreme love and admiration for themselves.
An egoist; a person full of egoism and pride.
An emotionally abusive parent that prioritizes their well-being over their children.
To a great extent or degree.
definitions.example: Expenses greatly exceeded revenues this year.

definitions.definition: Nobly; magnanimously.
Resembling, or characteristic of a gimmick.
Containing gimmicks.
Money received on payday as payment for work performed.
definitions.example: He was delighted after getting his first paycheck, but subsequently blew it all on the horses

definitions.definition: A prefix (honorific) or suffix (post-nominal) added to a person's name to signify either veneration, official position or a professional or academic qualification. See also
Legal right to ownership of a property; a deed or other certificate proving this.
definitions.example: a good title to an estate, or an imperfect title

definitions.definition: In canon law, that by which a beneficiary holds a benefice.
A church to which a priest was ordained, and where he was to reside.
The name of a book, film, musical piece, painting, or other work of art.
definitions.example: I know the singer's name, but not the title of the song.

definitions.definition: A publication.
definitions.example: Buyers of the new video game console can choose from three bundled titles.

definitions.definition: A section or division of a subject, as of a law or a book.
(chiefly in the plural) A written title, credit, or caption shown with a film, video, or performance.
definitions.example: The titles scrolled by too quickly to read.

definitions.definition: The panel for the name, between the bands of the back of a book.
The subject of a writing; a short phrase that summarizes the entire topic.
A division of an act of law
definitions.example: Title II of the USA PATRIOT Act

definitions.definition: The recognition given to the winner of a championship in sports.
To assign a title to; to entitle.
Glare from the road that can temporarily blind drivers.
A ladder; a series of steps; a means of ascending.
An ordered, usually numerical sequence used for measurement, means of assigning a magnitude.
definitions.example: Please rate your experience on a scale from 1 to 10.

definitions.definition: Size; scope.
definitions.example: The Holocaust was insanity on an enormous scale.

definitions.definition: The ratio of depicted distance to actual distance.
definitions.example: This map uses a scale of 1:10.

definitions.definition: A line or bar associated with a drawing, used to indicate measurement when the image has been magnified or reduced.
A series of notes spanning an octave, tritave, or pseudo-octave, used to make melodies.
A mathematical base for a numeral system; radix.
definitions.example: the decimal scale; the binary scale

definitions.definition: Gradation; succession of ascending and descending steps and degrees; progressive series; scheme of comparative rank or order.
A standard amount of money to be received by a performer or writer, negotiated by a union.
definitions.example: Sally wasn't the star of the show, so she was glad to be paid scale.

definitions.definition: To change the size of something whilst maintaining proportion; especially to change a process in order to produce much larger amounts of the final product.
definitions.example: We should scale that up by a factor of 10.

definitions.definition: To climb to the top of.
definitions.example: Hilary and Norgay were the first known to have scaled Everest.

definitions.definition: To tolerate significant increases in throughput or other potentially limiting factors.
definitions.example: That architecture won't scale to real-world environments.

definitions.definition: To weigh, measure or grade according to a scale or system.
A composer, conductor, or performer of music; specifically, a person who sings and/or plays a musical instrument as a hobby, an occupation, or a profession.
A liquid used to clean one's mouth.
To give permission, power, or the legal right to do something.
To give someone more confidence and/or strength to do something, often by enabling them to increase their control over their own life or situation.
definitions.example: John found that starting up his own business empowered him greatly in social situations.

definitions.definition: A man whose spouse has died (and who has not remarried); masculine of widow.
The ability to foresee or prepare wisely for the future.
definitions.example: Having the foresight to prepare an evacuation plan may have saved their lives.

definitions.definition: The front sight on a rifle or similar weapon
A bearing taken forwards towards a new object
A type of small boat, covered over by a surface deck, powered by the occupant or occupants using a double-bladed paddle in a sitting position, from a hole in the surface deck
To use a kayak, to travel or race in a kayak.
definitions.example: Kayaking is an Olympic sport.

definitions.definition: To traverse (a body of water) by kayak.
definitions.example: On a dare, he kayaked the Harlem River in New York from Hell's Gate to Spyten Duyvil.

definitions.definition: A wet nurse.
A person (usually a woman) who takes care of other people’s young.
definitions.example: They hired a nurse to care for their young boy.

definitions.definition: A person trained to provide care for the sick.
definitions.example: The nurse made her rounds through the hospital ward.

definitions.definition: One who, or that which, brings up, rears, causes to grow, trains, or fosters.
definitions.example: Eton College has been called "the chief nurse of England's statesmen".

definitions.definition: A shrub or tree that protects a young plant.
A lieutenant or first officer who takes command when the captain is unfit for his place.
A larva of certain trematodes, which produces cercariae by asexual reproduction.
A nurse shark.
To breastfeed: to feed (a baby) at the breast; to suckle.
definitions.example: She believes that nursing her baby will make him strong and healthy.

definitions.definition: To breastfeed: to be fed at the breast.
To care for (someone), especially in sickness; to tend to.
definitions.example: She nursed him back to health.

definitions.definition: To treat kindly and with extra care
definitions.example: She nursed the rosebush and that season it bloomed.

definitions.definition: To manage with care and economy
To drink slowly
To foster, to nourish
To hold closely to one's chest
definitions.example: Would you like to nurse the puppy?

definitions.definition: To strike (billiard balls) gently, so as to keep them in good position during a series of shots.
The position of the body or way of carrying oneself.
definitions.example: The ballet dancer walked with a graceful attitude.

definitions.definition: Disposition or state of mind.
definitions.example: Don't give me your negative attitude.

definitions.definition: The orientation of a vehicle or other object relative to the horizon, direction of motion, other objects, etc.
definitions.example: The airliner had to land with a nose-up attitude after the incident.

definitions.definition: A position similar to arabesque, but with the raised leg bent at the knee.
To assume or to place in a particular position or orientation; to pose.
To express an attitude through one's posture, bearing, tone of voice, etc.
A competitor thought unlikely to win.
Somebody at a disadvantage.
A high swing wherein the person pushing the swing runs beneath the swing while the person being pushed is at the forward limit of the arc.
Lacking a definition or value.
That does not have a meaning and is thus not assigned an interpretation.
definitions.example: The result of division by zero is undefined.

definitions.definition: Something that is unusual; an anomaly.
Not usual, out of the ordinary
definitions.example: Every once in a while, though, Idol comes across an unusual talent who just blows everyone away.

definitions.definition: A set amount of travelling, seen as a single unit; a discrete trip, a voyage.
Any process or progression likened to a journey, especially one that involves difficulties or personal development.
definitions.example: my journey of dealing with grief

definitions.definition: A day.
A day's travelling; the distance travelled in a day.
A day's work.
The weight of finished coins delivered at one time to the Master of the Mint.
(collective) A group of giraffes.
To travel, to make a trip or voyage.
Dissatisfied and irritable.
definitions.example: The old man was grumpy because he couldn’t find his glasses.

definitions.definition: Rough or unhusked rice, either before it is milled or as a crop to be harvested.
A paddy field, a rice paddy; an irrigated or flooded field where rice is grown.
The process of childbearing; the beginning of life.
An instance of childbirth.
definitions.example: Intersex babies account for roughly one per cent of all births.

definitions.definition: A beginning or start; a point of origin.
definitions.example: the birth of an empire

definitions.definition: The circumstances of one's background, ancestry, or upbringing.
definitions.example: He was of noble birth, but fortune had not favored him.

definitions.definition: That which is born.
A commodity; see realty.
(grammar) One of the three genders that the common gender can be separated into in the Scandinavian languages.
A real number.
A realist.
True, genuine, not merely nominal or apparent.
Genuine, not artificial, counterfeit, or fake.
definitions.example: This is real leather.

definitions.definition: Genuine, unfeigned, sincere.
definitions.example: These are real tears!

definitions.definition: Actually being, existing, or occurring; not fictitious or imaginary.
definitions.example: a description of real life

definitions.definition: That has objective, physical existence.
definitions.example: No one has ever seen a real unicorn.

definitions.definition: Having been adjusted to remove the effects of inflation; measured in purchasing power (contrast nominal).
definitions.example: My dad calculated my family's real consumption per month.

definitions.definition: Relating to the result of the actions of rational agents; relating to neoclassical economic models as opposed to Keynesian models.

(of a number) Being either a rational number, or the limit of a convergent infinite sequence of rational numbers: being one of a set of numbers with a one-to-one correspondence to the points on a line.
Relating to immovable tangible property.
definitions.example: real estate;  real property

definitions.definition: Absolute, complete, utter.
definitions.example: This is a real problem.

definitions.definition: Signifying meritorious qualities or actions especially as regard the enjoyment of life, prowess at sports, or success wooing potential partners.
definitions.example: I'm keeping it real.

definitions.definition: Really, very.
definitions.example: When I told him the truth, he got real mad.

definitions.definition: The act of retiring, or the state of being retired.
A place to which one retires.
A call sounded on a bugle, announcing to skirmishers that they are to retire, or fall back.
definitions.example: At the retire, the cavalry fell back.

definitions.definition: To stop working on a permanent basis, usually because of old age or illness.
definitions.example: Having made a large fortune, he retired.

definitions.definition: (sometimes reflexive) To withdraw; to take away.
To cease use or production of something.
definitions.example: The steamship made thousands of trips over several decades before it was retired by the shipping company.

definitions.definition: To withdraw from circulation, or from the market; to take up and pay.
definitions.example: The central bank retired those notes five years ago.

definitions.definition: To cause to retire; specifically, to designate as no longer qualified for active service; to place on the retired list.
definitions.example: The board retired the old major.

definitions.definition: (of a batsman) To voluntarily stop batting before being dismissed so that the next batsman can bat.
definitions.example: Jones retired in favour of Smith.

definitions.definition: (of a fielder) To make a play which results in a runner or the batter being out, either by means of a put out, fly out or strikeout.
definitions.example: Jones retired Smith 6-3.

definitions.definition: To go back or return; to withdraw or retreat, especially from public view; to go into privacy.
definitions.example: I will retire to the study.

definitions.definition: To retreat from action or danger; to withdraw for safety or pleasure.
definitions.example: The regiment retired from the fray after the Major was killed.

definitions.definition: To recede; to fall or bend back.
definitions.example: Past the point, the shore retires into a sequence of coves.

definitions.definition: To go to bed.
definitions.example: I will retire for the night.

definitions.definition: To remove or cease to use.
definitions.example: When a hurricane becomes so deadly or destructive that future use would be insensitive, officials may retire the name of the hurricane.

definitions.definition: A course of violent, frenzied action.
To move about wildly or violently.
A species of mallow, Althaea officinalis, that grows in marshy terrain.

A type of confectionery, originally (since Ancient Egyptian times) made from this plant, but now generally made of sugar or corn syrup, gelatin that has been pre-softened in water, gum arabic, flavorings, and sometimes beaten egg whites, all whipped to a spongy consistency.
Someone who is soft and benign.
definitions.example: He acts like a total badass, but he's really just a marshmallow once you get to know him.

definitions.definition: To soften (literally or figuratively)
A specific form or variation of something.
A translation from one language to another.
definitions.example: It's only in the King James Version of the Bible.

definitions.definition: A school exercise, generally of composition in a foreign language.
The act of translating, or rendering, from one language into another language.
An account or description from a particular point of view, especially as contrasted with another account.
definitions.example: He gave another version of the affair.

definitions.definition: A particular revision (of software, firmware, CPU, etc.).
definitions.example: Upgrade to the latest version for new features and bug fixes.

definitions.definition: A condition of the uterus in which its axis is deflected from its normal position without being bent upon itself. See anteversion and retroversion.
An eye movement involving both eyes moving synchronously and symmetrically in the same direction.
A change of form, direction, etc.; transformation; conversion.
definitions.example: External cephalic version is a process by which a breech baby can sometimes be turned from buttocks or foot first to head first.

definitions.definition: To keep track of (a file, document, etc.) in a versioning system.
A wheeled vehicle that moves independently, with at least three wheels, powered mechanically, steered by a driver and mostly for personal transportation.
definitions.example: She drove her car to the mall.

definitions.definition: A wheeled vehicle, drawn by a horse or other animal; a chariot.
An unpowered unit in a railroad train.
definitions.example: The conductor coupled the cars to the locomotive.

definitions.definition: An individual vehicle, powered or unpowered, in a multiple unit.
definitions.example: The 11:10 to London was operated by a 4-car diesel multiple unit.

definitions.definition: A passenger-carrying unit in a subway or elevated train, whether powered or not.
definitions.example: From the frontmost car of the subway, he filmed the progress through the tunnel.

definitions.definition: A rough unit of quantity approximating the amount which would fill a railroad car.
definitions.example: We ordered five hundred cars of gypsum.

definitions.definition: The moving, load-carrying component of an elevator or other cable-drawn transport mechanism.
definitions.example: Fix the car of the express elevator - the door is sticking.

definitions.definition: The passenger-carrying portion of certain amusement park rides, such as Ferris wheels.
definitions.example: The most exciting part of riding a Ferris wheel is when your car goes over the top.

definitions.definition: The part of an airship, such as a balloon or dirigible, which houses the passengers and control apparatus.
A sliding fitting that runs along a track.
The aggregate of desirable characteristics of a car.
definitions.example: Buy now! You can get more car for your money.

definitions.definition: A floating perforated box for living fish.
A turn.
The first part of a cons in LISP. The first element of a list

An atmospheric phenomenon created by charged particles from the sun striking the upper atmosphere, creating coloured lights in the sky. It is usually named australis or borealis based on whether it is in the Southern or Northern Hemisphere respectively.
The action of adjusting something
The result of adjusting something; a small change; a minor correction; a modification or alteration
The settling or balancing of a financial account
definitions.example: The credit card company made an adjustment to my account to waive the late fee.

definitions.definition: The behavioural process of balancing conflicting needs, or needs against obstacles in the environment.
definitions.example: When Jim graduated, he found adjustment to the working world difficult.

definitions.definition: The assessment, by an insurance company, of a claim; the settlement of such a claim
Our universe, the sum of everything that exists in the cosmos, including time and space itself.
definitions.example: Powerful telescopes look far back into the distant reaches of the Universe.

definitions.definition: The sum of everything that exists in the cosmos, including time and space itself.
definitions.example: I think that the universe was created by a life force rather than a deity.

definitions.definition: An entity similar to our universe; one component of a larger entity known as the multiverse.
Everything under consideration.
definitions.example: In all this universe of possibilities, there is only one feasible option.

definitions.definition: A sample taken from the population.
An imaginary collection of worlds.
definitions.example: The universe in this comic book series is richly imagined.

definitions.definition: (films) A collection of stories with characters and settings that are less interrelated than those of sequels or prequels.
A whole world, in the sense of perspective or social setting.
definitions.example: That didn’t just rock my world, it rocked my universe.

definitions.definition: Any of several large North American hares of the genus Lepus
To make a sudden, rapid movement
A long, narrow channel or depression; e.g., such a slot cut into a hard material to provide a location for an engineering component, a tyre groove, or a geological channel or depression.
A fixed routine.
The middle of the strike zone in baseball where a pitch is most easily hit.
A pronounced, enjoyable rhythm.
A shaft or excavation.
A racing line, a path across the racing circuit's surface that a racecar will usually track on. (Note: There may be multiple grooves on any particular circuit or segment of circuit)
To cut a groove or channel in; to form into channels or grooves; to furrow.
To perform, dance to, or enjoy rhythmic music.
definitions.example: I was just starting to groove to the band when we had to leave.

definitions.definition: A monocotyledonous plant (Allium cepa), allied to garlic, used as vegetable and spice.
The bulb of such a plant.
The genus as a whole.
A ball.
A person from Bermuda or of Bermudian descent.

A platform mounted on two shafts, or a more elaborate construction, designed to be carried by two (or more) people to transport one (in luxury models sometimes more) third person(s) or (occasionally in the elaborate version) a cargo, such as a religious idol.
(collective) The offspring of a mammal born in one birth.
Material used as bedding for animals.

definitions.definition: Collectively, items discarded on the ground.
definitions.example: drop litter

definitions.definition: Absorbent material used in an animal's litter tray
definitions.example: the cat's litter

definitions.definition: Layer of fallen leaves and similar organic matter in a forest floor.
A covering of straw for plants.
To drop or throw trash without properly disposing of it (as discarding in public areas rather than trash receptacles).
definitions.example: By tossing the bottle out the window, he was littering.

definitions.definition: To scatter carelessly about.
To strew (a place) with scattered articles.
To give birth to, used of animals.
To produce a litter of young.
To supply (cattle etc.) with litter; to cover with litter, as the floor of a stall.
To be supplied with litter as bedding; to sleep or make one's bed in litter.
A field, stadium or park where ball, especially baseball, is played.
The general vicinity; somewhere close; a broad approximation.
definitions.example: Let’s get in the ballpark, then worry about the fine details.

definitions.definition: To make a rough estimate of.
definitions.example: If you don’t have the exact expense total, just ballpark it.

definitions.definition: Approximate; close; on the right order of magnitude.
A road that passes around something, such as a residential area
A circumvention
A section of pipe that conducts a fluid around some other fixture
An electrical shunt
An alternative passage created to divert a bodily fluid around a damaged organ; the surgical procedure to construct such a bypass
To avoid an obstacle etc, by constructing or using a bypass
To ignore the usual channels or procedures

An infectious disease caused by species of Lyssavirus that causes acute encephalitis in warm-blooded animals and people, characterised by abnormal behaviour such as biting, excitement, aggressiveness, and dementia, followed by paralysis and death.

Any species of the extinct genus Mammuthus, of large, usually hairy, elephant-like mammals with long curved tusks and an inclined back, which became extinct with the last retreat of ice age glaciers during the late Pleistocene period, and are known from fossils, frozen carcasses, and Paleolithic cave paintings found in North America and Eurasia.
A mastodon.
Something very large of its kind.
Comparable to a mammoth in its size; very large, huge, gigantic.
Medicine to be administered to the ear.
A pendant for the ear; an earring.
A plant of the genus Ehrendorferia in the family Papaveraceae, native to California.
To construct or form a web.
To cover with a web or network.
To ensnare or entangle.
To provide with a web.
To weave.
(of feet or hands) With the digits connected by a thin membrane.
Resembling a web.
Connected to the World Wide Web.
A person who is part machine, a robot who is part organic.
A robot who has an organic past.
A human with electronic or bionic prostheses.
The quality of being a craftsman.
An example of a craftsman's work.
definitions.example: The craftsmanship on the antique chair was exquisite.

definitions.definition: A line or vector that is perpendicular to another line, surface, or plane.
A person who is normal, who fits into mainstream society, as opposed to those who live alternative lifestyles.
The usual state.
definitions.example: Heavy workload is the new normal.

definitions.definition: Not swept
A thing that has physical existence.
Objective; the goal, end or purpose of something.
(grammar) The noun phrase which is an internal complement of a verb phrase or a prepositional phrase. In a verb phrase with a transitive action verb, it is typically the receiver of the action.
A person or thing toward which an emotion is directed.
definitions.example: Mary Jane had been the object of Peter's affection for years.

definitions.definition: An instantiation of a class or structure.
An element within a category upon which functions operate. Thus, a category consists of a set of element objects and the functions that operate on them.
Sight; show; appearance; aspect.
A spherical body; a globe; especially, one of the celestial spheres; a sun, planet, or star
One of the azure transparent spheres conceived by the ancients to be enclosed one within another, and to carry the heavenly bodies in their revolutions
A circle; especially, a circle, or nearly circular orbit, described by the revolution of a heavenly body; an orbit
A period of time marked off by the revolution of a heavenly body.
The eye, as luminous and spherical
A revolving circular body; a wheel
A sphere of action.
A globus cruciger; a ceremonial sphere used to represent royal power
A translucent sphere appearing in flash photography (Orb (optics))
A body of soldiers drawn up in a circle, as for defence, especially infantry to repel cavalry.
A blank window or panel.
A place or situation resembling Hell.
A large fire, a conflagration.
A soldier of the lowest rank in the army.
A doctor working in privately rather than publicly funded health care.
(in the plural) The genitals.
A secret message; a personal unofficial communication.
Personal interest; particular business.
Privacy; retirement.
One not invested with a public office.
(usually in the plural) A private lesson.

definitions.definition: Belonging to, concerning, or accessible only to an individual person or a specific group.
definitions.example: Her address is private; you can't have it.

definitions.definition: Not accessible by the public.
definitions.example: private property

definitions.definition: Not in governmental office or employment.
definitions.example: He quit public life, living quietly as a private citizen.

definitions.definition: Not publicly known; not open; secret.
definitions.example: The identity of the beneficiaries of the trust is private.

definitions.definition: Protected from view or disturbance by others; secluded.
definitions.example: Can we go somewhere more private?

definitions.definition: Not traded by the public.
definitions.example: private corporation

definitions.definition: Secretive; reserved.
definitions.example: He is a very private person.

definitions.definition: (of a room in a medical facility) Not shared with another patient.
Accessible only to the class itself or instances of it, and not to other classes or even subclasses.
A distressing or dangerous situation.
definitions.example: He was in trouble when the rain started.

definitions.definition: A difficulty, problem, condition, or action contributing to such a situation.
definitions.example: The trouble was a leaking brake line.   The trouble with that suggestion is that we lack the funds to put it in motion.   The bridge column magnified the trouble with a slight tilt in the wrong direction.

definitions.definition: A violent occurrence or event.
definitions.example: the troubles in Northern Ireland

definitions.definition: Efforts taken or expended, typically beyond the normal required.
definitions.example: It's no trouble for me to edit it.

definitions.definition: A malfunction.
definitions.example: He's been in hospital with some heart trouble.   My old car has engine trouble.

definitions.definition: Liability to punishment; conflict with authority.
definitions.example: He had some trouble with the law.

definitions.definition: A fault or interruption in a stratum.
To disturb, stir up, agitate (a medium, especially water).
To mentally distress; to cause (someone) to be anxious or perplexed.
definitions.example: What she said about narcissism is troubling me.

definitions.definition: In weaker sense: to bother or inconvenience.
definitions.example: I will not trouble you to deliver the letter.

definitions.definition: To take pains to do something.
definitions.example: I won't trouble to post the letter today; I can do it tomorrow.

definitions.definition: To worry; to be anxious.
(Australian rhyming slang) Wife.
To make a slow or arduous journey.
To journey on foot, especially to hike through mountainous areas.
To travel by ox wagon.
Capable of doing many things competently.
Having varied uses or many functions.
Changeable or inconstant.
Capable of moving freely in all directions.
(BDSM) Being a switch; capable of taking either a dominant or a submissive role.

definitions.definition: Capable of taking both a top and bottom role.
A usually alphabetized and explained collection of words e.g. of a particular field, or prepared for a specific purpose, often for learning.
The collection of words a person knows and uses.

definitions.definition: The stock of words used in a particular field.
definitions.example: The vocabulary of social sciences is often incomprehensible to ordinary people.

definitions.definition: The words of a language collectively.
definitions.example: The vocabulary of any language is influenced by contacts with other cultures.

definitions.definition: A range of artistic or stylistic forms or techniques
An unsteady motion.
definitions.example: The fat man walked down the street with a wobble.

definitions.definition: A tremulous sound.
definitions.example: There was a wobble on her high notes.

definitions.definition: A low-frequency oscillation sometimes used in dubstep
To move with an uneven or rocking motion, or unsteadily to and fro.
definitions.example: the Earth wobbles slowly on its axis;  the jelly wobbled on the plate

definitions.definition: To tremble or quaver.
definitions.example: The soprano's voice wobbled alarmingly.

definitions.definition: To vacillate in one's opinions.
definitions.example: I'm wobbling between the Liberals and the Greens.

definitions.definition: To cause to wobble.
definitions.example: The boy wobbled the girl's bike.

definitions.definition: Distant.

definitions.definition: Not mentally present, as when daydreaming.

definitions.definition: An emission of digestive gases from the anus; a flatus.
(impolite) An irritating person; a fool.
(impolite, potentially offensive) (usually as "old fart") An elderly person; especially one perceived to hold old-fashioned views.
(impolite) To emit digestive gases from the anus; to flatulate.
(usually as "fart around") To waste time with idle and inconsequential tasks; to go about one's activities in a lackadaisical manner; to be lazy or over-relaxed in one's manner or bearing.
To emit (fumes, gases, etc.).
Mental apprehension of whatever may be known, thought, or imagined; idea, concept.
A sentiment; an opinion.
Sense; mind.
An invention; an ingenious device; a knickknack.

definitions.definition: Any small article used in sewing and haberdashery, either for attachment to garments or as a tool, such as a button, zipper, or thimble.
Inclination; intention; disposition.

definitions.definition: Unfortunate, marked by misfortune.
Inauspicious.
Having ill luck.
Bringing ill luck.
To pull something sharply; to pull something out

definitions.definition: To take or remove (someone) quickly from a particular place or situation.
To gently play a single string, e.g. on a guitar, violin etc.

definitions.definition: To remove feathers from a bird.
To rob, fleece, steal forcibly
definitions.example: The horny highwayman plucked his victims to their underwear, or attractive ones all the way.

definitions.definition: To play a string instrument pizzicato.
definitions.example: Plucking a bow instrument may cause a string to break.

definitions.definition: To pull or twitch sharply.
definitions.example: to pluck at somebody's sleeve

definitions.definition: (university slang) To be rejected after failing an examination for a degree.
Of a glacier: to transport individual pieces of bedrock by means of gradual erosion through freezing and thawing.
(of something with feathers, hair etc.) Having had these items removed by plucking.
(of the strings of an instrument) Played by plucking.
Having courage and spirit; plucky.
The region which the eye overlooks at one time; view; scene; outlook.
A picturesque or panoramic view; a landscape; hence, a sketch of a landscape.
A position affording a fine view; a lookout.
Relative position of the front of a building or other structure; face; relative aspect.
The act of looking forward; foresight; anticipation.
The potential things that may come to pass, often favorable.
A hope; a hopeful.
Any player whose rights are owned by a top-level professional team, but who has yet to play a game for said team.
(sales) A potential client or customer.
The façade of an organ.
To search, as for gold.
To determine which minerals or metals are present in a location.
Disbelief or uncertainty (about something); a particular instance of such disbelief or uncertainty.

definitions.definition: A point of uncertainty; a query.
To be undecided about; to lack confidence in; to disbelieve, to question.
definitions.example: He doubted that was really what you meant.

definitions.definition: To harbour suspicion about; suspect.
To anticipate with dread or fear; to apprehend.
To fill with fear; to affright.
To dread, to fear.
A telecommunication device (originally mechanical, and now electronic) used for two-way talking with another person (now often shortened to phone).
The game of Chinese whispers.
To (attempt to) contact someone using the telephone.
To convey (a message) by telephoning.
An ethical drug, one only dispensed on the prescription of a physician.
Of or relating to the study of ethics.
definitions.example: The philosopher Kant is particularly known for his ethical writings.

definitions.definition: Of or relating to the accepted principles of right and wrong, especially those of some organization or profession.
definitions.example: All employees must familiarize themselves with our ethical guidelines.

definitions.definition: Morally approvable; good.
definitions.example: We are trying to decide what the most ethical course of action would be.

definitions.definition: (of a drug) Only dispensed on the prescription of a physician.
definitions.example: In most jurisdictions, morphine is classified as an ethical drug.

definitions.definition: Any crystalline substance.
Aniline
Of, relating to, or composed of crystals.
Having a regular three-dimensional molecular structure.
Resembling crystal in being clear and transparent.
Of an umber colour.
An adult castrated male of cattle (B. taurus).
Any bovine animal (genus Bos). A neat, a beef.
Of, relating to, or resembling an ox; oxlike
A banned or unlawful item.
Not approved by law, but not invalid.
Breaking social norms.
Unlawful.
The condition of not being threatened, especially physically, psychologically, emotionally, or financially.
Something that secures.
An organization or department responsible for providing security by enforcing laws, rules, and regulations as well as maintaining order.
Something that secures the fulfillment of an obligation or law.
Freedom from apprehension.

Proof of ownership of stocks, bonds or other investment instruments.
Property etc. temporarily relinquished to guarantee repayment of a loan.
A guarantee.
Carelessness; negligence.
Membership of a particular nation or state, by origin, birth, naturalization, ownership, allegiance or otherwise.
National, i.e. ethnic and/or cultural, character or identity.
A people sharing a common origin, culture and/or language, and possibly constituting a nation-state.
Political existence, independence or unity as a national entity.
Nationalism or patriotism.
Collective term for the capital letters A, B, C, ... as opposed to the small letters a, b, c, ....
To convert (text) to upper case.
Written in upper case; capital
One who owns something.

definitions.definition: The captain of a ship.
To assign initial values to something
To assign an initial value to a variable
To format a storage medium prior to use
To prepare any hardware (such as a printer or scanner) for use
To wound or cause physical harm to a living creature.
To damage or impair.
To do injustice to.
The action of the verb talk.
The realm of faerie; enchantment, illusion.

A mythical being with magical powers, known in many sizes and descriptions, although often depicted in modern illustrations only as a small sprite with gauze-like wings, and revered in some modern forms of paganism.
An enchantress, or creature of overpowering charm.
A male homosexual, especially one who is effeminate.
A member of two species of hummingbird in the genus Heliothryx.
One who plays the fiddle.
One who fiddles.
A burrowing crab of the genus Gelasimus, of many species. The male has one claw very much enlarged, and often holds it in a position similar to that in which a musician holds a fiddle.
The common European sandpiper (Actitis hypoleucos); so called because it continually oscillates its body.
(of a person) Being or like a nerd.
definitions.example: That guy is nerdy and weird.

definitions.definition: (of a quality or interest) Of, pertaining to, in the style of, or appealing to nerds.
definitions.example: I got a pair of nerdy glasses and clothes for Halloween.

definitions.definition: The transpose of the cofactor matrix of a given square matrix.
(of a matrix) Transpose conjugate.
(of an operator) Hermitian conjugate.
A functor related to another functor by an adjunction.

A curve A such that any point of a given curve C of multiplicity r has multiplicity at least r–1 on A. Sometimes the multiple points of C are required to be ordinary, and if this condition is not satisfied the term sub-adjoint is used.
An assistant to someone who holds a position in the military or civil service.
An assistant mayor of a French commune.
Used in certain contexts, in each case involving a pair of transformations, one of which is, or is analogous to, conjugation (either inner automorphism or complex conjugation).
(of a functor) That is related to another functor by an adjunction.
(of one curve to another curve) Having a relationship of the nature of an adjoint (adjoint curve); sharing multiple points with.
The inevitable progression into the future with the passing of present and past events.
definitions.example: Time stops for nobody.   the ebb and flow of time

definitions.definition: A duration of time.
An instant of time.
The measurement under some system of region of day or moment.
definitions.example: Let's synchronize our watches so we're not on different time.

definitions.definition: Ratio of comparison.
definitions.example: your car runs three times faster than mine;  that is four times as heavy as this

definitions.definition: (grammar) Tense.
definitions.example: the time of a verb

definitions.definition: The measured duration of sounds; measure; tempo; rate of movement; rhythmical division.
definitions.example: common or triple time;   the musician keeps good time.

definitions.definition: To measure or record the time, duration, or rate of.
definitions.example: I used a stopwatch to time myself running around the block.

definitions.definition: To choose when something begins or how long it lasts.
definitions.example: The President timed his speech badly, coinciding with the Super Bowl.

definitions.definition: To keep or beat time; to proceed or move in time.
To pass time; to delay.
To regulate as to time; to accompany, or agree with, in time of movement.
To measure, as in music or harmony.
Reminder by the umpire for the players to continue playing after their pause.
The umpire's call in prizefights, etc.
A call by a bartender to warn patrons that the establishment is closing and no more drinks will be served.
Showbusiness
A small fragment or share of something, commonly applied to food.
A mouthful of food.
A very small amount.
